executable_code.code:
import pandas as pd
import json

df = pd.read_csv("input_file_0.csv", sep=",", encoding="utf-8")

# Convert types
numeric_cols = ["BIOLOGY", "CHEMISTRY", "ENGLISH", "FATHER_AGE", "HISTORY", "LITERATURE", "MATH", "MOTHER_AGE", "PHYSICS", "STUDENT_ID", "AGE"]
for col in numeric_cols:
    if col in df.columns:
        if df[col].dtype == object:
            df[col] = df[col].astype(str).str.replace(',', '.')
        df[col] = pd.to_numeric(df[col], errors='coerce')

# Filter
df_filtered = df[(df['AGE'] >= 15) & (df['AGE'] <= 20) & (df['FATHER_JOB'].notna()) & (df['FATHER_JOB'].str.strip() != '')]

# Group by
result = df_filtered.groupby(['RESIDENTIAL_AREA', 'FATHER_JOB']).agg(
    avg_age=('AGE', 'mean'),
    count_students=('STUDENT_ID', 'count'),
    last_student_id=('STUDENT_ID', 'last')
).reset_index()

# Format float to 2 decimal places
result['avg_age'] = result['avg_age'].round(2)

print(json.dumps(result.values.tolist(), ensure_ascii=False))

executable_code.code:
import pandas as pd
import json

df = pd.read_csv("input_file_0.csv", sep=",", encoding="utf-8")

for col in df.columns:
    if col in ["BIOLOGY", "CHEMISTRY", "ENGLISH", "FATHER_AGE", "HISTORY", "LITERATURE", "MATH", "MOTHER_AGE", "PHYSICS", "STUDENT_ID", "AGE"]:
        if df[col].dtype == object:
            df[col] = df[col].astype(str).str.replace(',', '.')
        df[col] = pd.to_numeric(df[col], errors='coerce')

df_filtered = df[(df['AGE'] >= 15) & (df['AGE'] <= 20) & df['FATHER_JOB'].notna() & (df['FATHER_JOB'].str.strip() != "")]

result = df_filtered.groupby(['RESIDENTIAL_AREA', 'FATHER_JOB']).agg(
    avg_age=('AGE', 'mean'),
    count_students=('STUDENT_ID', 'count'),
    last_student_id=('STUDENT_ID', 'last')
).reset_index()

result['avg_age'] = result['avg_age'].round(2)

print(json.dumps(result.values.tolist(), ensure_ascii=False))

parts.text:
```python
import pandas as pd
import json

# Read the dataset
df = pd.read_csv("input_file_0.csv", sep=",", encoding="utf-8")

# Data cleaning and type conversion based on dataset rules
numeric_columns = ["BIOLOGY", "CHEMISTRY", "ENGLISH", "FATHER_AGE", "HISTORY", "LITERATURE", "MATH", "MOTHER_AGE", "PHYSICS", "STUDENT_ID", "AGE"]
for col in numeric_columns:
    if col in df.columns:
        if df[col].dtype == object:
            df[col] = df[col].astype(str).str.replace(',', '.')
        df[col] = pd.to_numeric(df[col], errors='coerce')

# Filter for students aged 15 to 20 with father's job information available
filtered_df = df[(df['AGE'] >= 15) & (df['AGE'] <= 20) & (df['FATHER_JOB'].notna()) & (df['FATHER_JOB'].str.strip() != "")]

# Group by residential area and father's job
result = filtered_df.groupby(['RESIDENTIAL_AREA', 'FATHER_JOB']).agg(
    AGE_mean=('AGE', 'mean'),
    COUNT=('STUDENT_ID', 'count'),
    LAST_ID=('STUDENT_ID', 'last')
).reset_index()

# Round floats to 2 decimal places
result['AGE_mean'] = result['AGE_mean'].round(2)

# Convert output to JSON list of lists
print(json.dumps(result.values.tolist(), ensure_ascii=False))
```
